Option B is telling someone to *keep* doing this and then says "good idea." So the structure is: "Keep doing X — good idea." Again, straightforward, like the speaker is suggesting that continuing this behavior is a good idea. Now, to determine which one is sarcastic. In this case, if someone is actually advising against putting people into compartments (option A) and calls it a "good idea," that would be a straightforward, sincere statement.
